content.full_text:
DAVID B. PARMELEE
Attorney At Law
1624 Tyler Street
Berkeley, California 94703
(415) 644-1548


December 5, 1990


Ms. Karen Haney-Owens, Associate Planner
Planning Department
THE CITY OF BERKELEY
2180 Milvia Street, 2nd Floor
Berkeley, CA 94704


Dear Ms. Haney-Owens:

     Please let this letter confirm our conversation on
Thursday, November 29, 1990, wherein you agreed to set up a
meeting in the very near future to discuss the down-zoning in
District Three (3). As you know, we have been attending in
good faith since February, 1990 to have this issue decided. The
Planning Commission's latest maneuver is perceived by many to be
the "last straw" in a pattern of bad faith actions on behalf of
the City.

     We agreed that a meeting among interested and concerned
parties may function to stave-off any intended litigation. To
that end, we request that representatives of the mayor's office,
city manager's office and city attorney's office be in
attendance. We would also expect the attendance of an approved
Planning Commission member. Commissioner Patrick Daveggio is
quite familiar with this issue and we would be pleased with his
input. Our group will be represented by Deborah Caldon and
myself. Once we have set a date, we will also seek
representatives' attendance from other neighborhood groups in
the impacted area. We will expect to hear from you in the near
future with possible dates for this meeting.

Ms. Karen Haney-Owens
December 5, 1990
Page 2


You informed me that it would be politically expedient to
request that Planning Director Gil Kelly attend this meeting.
We are formally stating that we have no interest in Mr. Kelly's
input. We inform you that a major basis of any lawsuit against
the City over this issue is Mr. Kelly's perceived and continued
lack of good faith in dealing with our group. We have come to
understand that it was at Mr. Kelly's directive that none of our
correspondence of Spring, 1990 was responded to; we believe this
actionable in itself and at the very least extremely
inappropriate for a professional in his position. We certainly
do not approve of his behavior in any manner during the course
of our dealing with the planning department.

We find further evidence of Mr. Kelly's lack of good
faith with regard to our group in that he was on the phone to
Patrick Devaney approximately two to three hours after my
conversation with you. It appears the purpose of this
conversation was to try and "cut a deal" on this vital
neighborhood issue. We believe that this smacks of the above-
referenced bad faith. We do not believe that we are being
unreasonable in our request that Mr. Kelly no longer be involved
in this process.

We strongly suggest before the meeting that you create,
on the City's behalf, a chronological time line of the City's
actions (inactions ?) regarding our concerns since we first
raised the issue in February, 1990. Perhaps it will put your
reliance on non-compliance with Robert's Rules of Orders in the
proper perspective with regard to the City's lack of good faith.

Under the Freedom of Information Act, we are formally
requesting that you immediately forward to us any and all
documents, notes, memoranda, etc. concerning the investigation
of the Planning Commission's original vote. We specifically
request the names of those individuals on the Planning
Commission or not who requested the City Attorney investigate
the vote.

Further, on December 4, 1990, I received your Notice of
Special Planning Commission Meeting. This is to inform you that
our group considers this to be insufficient notice, under
California law, regarding the tour referenced on Friday,
December 7, 1990. We again are surprised by your actions in
"notifying" the public that they are invited to a tour that
begins at 10:00 a.m. on a business day when almost every
concerned citizen is working.

Ms. Karen Haney-Owens
December 5, 1990
Page 3


     We formally request that you reschedule this tour to a
weekend in the near future so that the Planning Commission can
receive active citizen input.  This, of course, by necessity,
and with proper notice, will cause the action to reclassify sub-
area three (3), scheduled for the next Planning Commission
meeting on December 12, 1990 to be rescheduled.

     Quite frankly, our group has talked to many citizens in
the impacted area and there is a sense of bewilderment and anger
at what is perceived to be another unilaterally political
decision by the City of Berkeley and its Planning Commission to
do something that clearly contravenes the wishes of the majority
of the affected citizens.  We hope the meeting, hereby formally
requested, will help to resolve this dispute in an amicable
manner and in a manner in which neighborhood input is for once
truly considered.

     We also requested of your office that a list of every
decision by the Planning Commission in which Max Anderson
participated in since his reappointment, be forwarded to the
above address.  As of this date, that information has not been
forthcoming.  It is our position that Mr. Anderson, especially
since having been found to have a conflict of interest by the
City Attorney, (and still under investigation by the FPPC) is
unfit to hold office on the Planning Commission.  We have
reviewed the Berkeley Commissioner's Manual and find no
guidance.

     By copy of this letter to the City Attorney, we are
formally requesting that this question be investigated.  Please
be assured that we will be conducting our own investigation of
the proper laws in this regard.  We believe it is important
enough to initiate the filing of another complaint with the FPPC
if the City Attorney or if the City of Berkeley is not
forthcoming with a decision that has an actual basis in law in
the near future.

     However, we believe the issue is important enough that
the City Attorney should direct Mr. Anderson to no longer
participate in any Planning Commission activities until his
position on that Commission is deemed legitimate and
appropriate.  We are also investigating his participation in the
Lorin Station Project for violations of city and state laws.

Ms. Karen Haney-Owens
December 5, 1990
Page 4


     Finally, please be assured that this letter is written
based on information and belief in a good faith attempt to try
to resolve these issues with the City.  We will expect that the
City will treat our concerns in a like manner and respond to
them in the very near and immediate future. Thank you for your
attention to these matters.


                                   Very truly yours,

                                   [signature]
                                   DAVID B. PARMELEE

DBP/lmy

cc:  The Honorable Loni Hancock
     Mayor of the City of Berkeley

     Mr. Michael Brown, City Manager
     City of Berkeley

     Manuela Albuquerque, City Attorney
     City of Berkeley

     Members of the Board of Planning Commission
     City of Berkeley

sections.question_synthetic: This is not an FPPC advice letter but rather correspondence from an attorney to a city planner regarding zoning disputes, planning commission procedures, and allegations of conflict of interest involving a planning commissioner.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This document is incoming correspondence (not an FPPC response letter). It references an FPPC investigation of Max Anderson but is not itself an FPPC advice letter. No question/conclusion sections present. Document mentions threat to file FPPC complaint but this is correspondence from a private attorney to city officials regarding local zoning/planning disputes.

embedding.summary: This is a December 5, 1990 letter from attorney David B. Parmelee to Berkeley Planning Department staff regarding ongoing disputes over District Three down-zoning, requesting a meeting with city officials and raising concerns about Planning Director Gil Kelly's conduct, insufficient public notice for a planning commission tour, and conflicts of interest involving Planning Commissioner Max Anderson who is under FPPC investigation.
